problem: Over the last 14 days, 12 Engineering Manager jobs reached the LLM scorer and were immediately rejected as wrong-function. The title_keywords exclusion list does not include 'engineering manager', so these jobs pass the hard filter and waste an LLM scoring call each. Across all-time data: 203 Engineering Manager jobs fetched, all rejected, 0 approvals ever.
hypothesis: Adding 'engineering manager' to criteria.exclusions.title_keywords via scripts/add-title-exclusions.ts will catch these jobs at the hard-filter stage (pre-LLM), eliminating ~12 wasted scoring calls per 14-day cycle. The includesAny(title, ex.title_keywords) check in backend/filters.ts uses substring matching so 'engineering manager' will match all variants ('Engineering Manager II', 'Senior Engineering Manager', etc.).

change.description: Add 'engineering manager' to NEW_EXCLUSIONS in scripts/add-title-exclusions.ts. After merge, operator runs 'npx tsx scripts/add-title-exclusions.ts' to apply the keyword to the live profile criteria_json.

proxy_results.quality_signals: The 12 jobs removed from the LLM queue are all Engineering Manager titles (confirmed by comparing baseline replay titles vs experiment replay titles). No PM titles are affected. 203 all-time EM rejections with 0 approvals confirms zero false-positive risk. The 188 jobs still passing the filter include 2 legitimate PM roles ('Product Manager, Verticals', 'Product Manager, Analytics') that remain surfaced.
proxy_results.agent_reasoning: Zero false-positive risk (203/203 EM historical rejections with 0 approvals), precise keyword with clear substring semantics, eliminates 12 wasted LLM calls per 14-day cycle. Cost savings are modest (~$0.03/cycle) but the structural improvement is sound. Tests pass.
